steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures around the blank
steps.content: Left of the blank: the opening elements of a proper noun (first and middle names). Right of the blank: the conclusion of the proper noun (“Kingelez”) followed immediately by the main verb (“were featured”) of the sentence. No clause boundary separates these elements.

steps.title: Apply the standard for names within a clause
steps.content:
Choice C (no punctuation) preserves the proper noun as a single, unbroken unit connecting to the verb.

A semicolon would falsely mark a clause boundary within parts of a name; a comma would interrupt the name's structural continuity; a colon would suggest the name introduces what follows.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
